content:
Net-new story gap table (continued — rows 19–36)

Story Gap Likelihood PM lens QA lens Dev lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)
HRREC-91995
PH: Recruiter gets discard popup by changing pages		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — Navigate away or close panel after edits confirm discard no backend drafts local only.
  • Salomon — Masked profile SMS obstruction HRREC-86124 MB overview.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Same epic peer slice.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): Slack unsaved notes discard modal Princess side panel patterns.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — unsaved changes discard search empty.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Jira scenarios cover the main recruiter journey; open questions remain on negative paths and tenant-specific configuration called out in lenses.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

Scenario: Blocked or unhappy path coverage for HRREC-91995

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile
When the system is in the unhappy path implied by this story title: PH: Recruiter gets discard popup by changing pages
Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with Jira

HRREC-91996
PH: Recruiter gets discard popup when they click the Discard button		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — Discard confirm empty no confirm Escape equals Discard.
  • Salomon — UPC confirmation Escape; UIPLATFORM RTE spurious required errors.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Same epic peer slice.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): Rich text App Builder threads low direct Discard match.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — discard draft search empty.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Jira scenarios cover the main recruiter journey; open questions remain on negative paths and tenant-specific configuration called out in lenses.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

HRREC-91997
PH: Recruiter sees list of messages with unread ones marked in blue		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — Unread blue markers refresh server read-state multi recruiter accessibility non-color-only.
  • Salomon — Cop recruiting delegation; color-blind palette notes.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84407: email read semantics differ from WhatsApp; state what you promise in the blue unread treatment versus what delivery or open receipts actually support.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Same epic peer slice.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): Customer incident unread blue SMS icon Drogon import.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — unread message recruiting search empty.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Jira scenarios cover the main recruiter journey; open questions remain on negative paths and tenant-specific configuration called out in lenses.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

PH: Style only - Recruiter sees messages showing, styled as per figma		Very LowGrey

No material gap beyond cosmetic governance.

  • Jira — UI-only thread styling Figma newest first.
  • Salomon — PII in email thread security consult Canvas Kit side panel tokens.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.

  • XO MCP — Empty conversational email thread search.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Style-only scope is narrow with limited engineering risk beyond token drift versus Figma.
  • Recommended next step: Pin Figma version IDs to the story before visual sign-off.

No additional BDD suggested beyond Jira’s styling scenario for this pass.

HRREC-92003
PH: Recruiter sees error messages if their email has bounced		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — Six bounce variants headline copy hard bounce spam unsubscribe virus delay generic.
  • Salomon — SES suppression CloudWatch bounce runbooks TRANMGMT translation note.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84406: classify bounce vs validation vs policy failures and surface recruiter-readable copy in the right surface (thread vs composer), not only on send.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Only self hit.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): No Slack hits.

HRREC-92004
PH: Recruiter sees error when sending message (REST error)		HighYellow

Material failure gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — Mid-session state change block generic 500 network draft loss risk.
  • Salomon — SendMessage REST failure UI pattern collection letter.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Only self hit.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): No Slack hits.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — send message recruiting API noisy generic security hits.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: REST and network failures during send can lose recruiter trust if drafts and retry paths are ambiguous.
  • Recommended next step: Align with engineering on idempotent send handling and whether drafts survive REST errors before QA hardens suites.

Scenario: REST error recover-forward

Given a recruiter composes a valid email on an active thread
When the first send attempt returns the REST error class described in Jira
Then the thread and composer show the agreed error, preserve or discard drafts per policy, and allow a safe retry path

HRREC-92005
PH: Recruiter sees info message and cannot send email if the candidate changes their email		HighYellow

Identity change gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — Primary home email change hides Reply Add info banner merge testing.
  • Salomon — Merge primary email duplicate management messaging panel HRREC-owned.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-90306 / merge identity: primary email change plus merge should not leave recruiters replying as the wrong person or reading a thread keyed to a stale address.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Only self hit.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): No Slack hits.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — merge primary email messaging generic document hits.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Primary home email changes mid-thread can strand recruiters unless info banners and read-only history match policy.
  • Recommended next step: Workshop merge plus email-change sequencing with engineering so pause rules match SMS precedents where intended.

Scenario: Candidate email change mid-thread

Given a recruiter has an active two-way email thread open on the candidate profile
When the candidate’s primary home email changes while the panel is still open
Then Reply and Add hide with the info copy from Jira and historical messages remain read-only without silent resend targets

HRREC-92012
PH: Admin can configure conversation lifecycle tenant setting?		HighYellow

Policy clarity gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — N days after disposition one year cap scheduler invalid N rejected.
  • Salomon — Tenant Setup Recruiting extend close SMS 180d disposition closure.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.

  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Open tenant policy story.
  • Salomon — Slack (paraphrase): No Slack hits.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — Empty key search.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Conversation lifecycle numbers and hard caps need validation UX that matches recruiting policy and scheduler behaviour.
  • Recommended next step: Facilitate one tenant-policy workshop covering invalid N, hard caps, and how closed threads surface in the panel.

Scenario: Invalid lifecycle configuration

Given a tenant administrator enters an out-of-range lifecycle value described in Jira
When they attempt to save the tenant setting
Then the tenant UI rejects the value with copy that matches recruiting policy and leaves prior behaviour unchanged
